steps.title: Fastest path: compare the side lengths
steps.content: Use the given side lengths first. Since two sides are already equal, the triangle is equilateral exactly when the third side has that same length.

steps.title: Recall the definition of an equilateral triangle
steps.content: An equilateral triangle has all three sides of equal length. To prove triangle PQR is equilateral, we must establish that PQ = QR = PR.

steps.title: Determine what PR must equal to satisfy the equilateral condition
steps.content: We are given PQ = 3,860 mm and QR = 3,860 mm. Since we need all three sides to be equal, PR must equal PQ and QR. Therefore, PR must equal 3,860 mm. That is the correct answer.
